stem: In the primitives model, what does the Sampler primitive fundamentally return for a circuit that ends in measurements?

options.text: Samples drawn from the circuit's output probability distribution — per-shot measurement bitstrings that you can histogram into (quasi-)probabilities
The exact complex amplitudes of the circuit's output statevector
Expectation values of a set of observables
The single most-likely measurement outcome of the circuit

explanation.correct: 'Sampling' means repeatedly executing the circuit and recording the measured bitstring each shot — draws from the distribution the circuit defines over computational-basis states. The Sampler returns those per-shot samples (as a BitArray); estimating probabilities is then just counting frequencies. It never returns amplitudes or expectation values.

distractors.B: Amplitudes are hidden by measurement — sampling only sees classical outcomes with probabilities |amplitude|^2. Statevectors are available from simulators (quantum_info), not from a hardware Sampler.
distractors.C: Expectation values of observables are the job of the Estimator primitive, not the Sampler.
distractors.D: The Sampler returns the full collection of shot outcomes, not a single winner; a distribution over many shots is the whole point.
